title: Non-Healing Wound - Skin Graft
chief_complaint: 55-year-old diabetic male with 4x3cm non-healing ulcer on left lower leg for 3 months. Failed conservative treatment with dressings. Good granulation tissue now present.

physical_exam.leg: 4x3cm ulcer with healthy granulation tissue, no purulence, no exposed bone or tendon, surrounding skin normal

correct_diagnosis: Chronic Non-Healing Leg Ulcer - Candidate for Split-Thickness Skin Graft
key_learning_points: STSG for wounds with good granulation tissue
Graft take requires: vascular bed, no infection, immobilization

patient_responses.wound: It's been there for 3 months, not getting smaller

feedback.correct_message: Well assessed! This wound is ready for STSG.
feedback.incorrect_message: This wound needs a skin graft. Good granulation tissue is the indication.
feedback.attending_note: STSG: Harvest from thigh (0.012-0.015 inch), mesh 1:1.5, apply to wound bed, bolster dressing for 5 days. Donor site: transparent film dressing. Post-op: leg elevation, offloading, compression after graft take. Optimize diabetes control.
